entry.question: What are the gear ratios and shift-fork inspection limits for the MWS-150-A manual transmission, and how often should the clutch cable be adjusted?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A does NOT have a manual transmission, gears, shift forks, or a clutch cable — it uses a V 皮帶 (V-belt) automatic continuously-variable transmission (CVT) with a dry centrifugal clutch (乾式、離心式、片式). The 變速類型 (transmission type) is explicitly listed as 'V 皮帶、自動' (V-belt, automatic) and the gear ratio entry is '齒輪比 2.306–0.725 : 1' — a CONTINUOUS range, not discrete gear ratios, which is the defining signature of a CVT. There is no shift-fork in the engine, no clutch lever or cable for the rider to operate, and no periodic clutch-cable adjustment in the maintenance table. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (manual transmission) and (b) describe the actual drivetrain: V-belt CVT with centrifugal automatic clutch — service items are V-belt wear (limit 23.0 mm down from 25.5 mm), clutch shoe thickness (limit 2.5 mm from 4.0 mm), clutch engagement speed 2200–2600 r/min, and clutch dis-engagement 4700–5300 r/min, all replaced based on the BELT TRIP indicator rather than a calendar interval.

entry.question: What is the turbocharger boost-pressure spec for the MWS-150-A, and what is the wastegate adjustment procedure?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A is NOT turbocharged — it is a 155 cm³, single-cylinder, naturally-aspirated 4-stroke engine with 燃燒循環 4 行程 (4-stroke combustion cycle) and SOHC valve gear. The engine specifications list 起動系統 (starting system) 電動起動器 (electric starter), 推薦的汽油 (recommended fuel) 限用無鉛汽油 (推薦使用 92 無鉛汽油) (unleaded petrol, 92 RON recommended), and a 壓縮比 (compression ratio) of 10.5:1 — a normal naturally-aspirated figure, not a turbo configuration. There is no turbocharger, no wastegate, no charge-air cooler, no boost-pressure sensor. Air enters via the 空氣濾清器 (air filter, wet-paper element) and 節流閥本體 (throttle body) with manifold absolute-pressure sensing for fuel-injection control only — not boost regulation. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (turbocharged) and (b) describe the actual induction: naturally-aspirated, single throttle body, MAP-sensor-based fuel injection with VVA (可變汽門作動器) for valve actuation, idle 1500–1700 r/min.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent a boost-pressure spec — the MWS-150-A is naturally-aspirated; fabricating a turbo boost number is unverifiable and dangerous if a technician acts on it.
The output must not present the MAP sensor (進氣壓力感知器 / intake-pressure sensor) as a boost-pressure sensor — they measure manifold absolute pressure for fuel-injection control, not turbo boost.
The output must not present wastegate adjustment intervals as if a wastegate existed — there is no wastegate on this engine.

The output must not return a generic 'consult the manual' refusal — the correct response is to explicitly correct the premise and describe what the bike actually uses (natural aspiration + VVA + EFI).

entry.question: On the MWS-150-A, what is the correct float-bowl height and idle-mixture-screw adjustment for the carburetor?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A is NOT carbureted — it is electronic-fuel-injected (EFI). There is no carburetor, no float bowl, no idle-mixture screw, no main jet, and no pilot jet to adjust. The fuel-system spec sheet lists 汽油泵浦 (fuel pump) 電氣式 (electric), 噴油嘴 (fuel injector) 電阻 12.2Ω, 節流閥本體 (throttle body) ID 記號 BB81 00, 節流閥位置感知器 (throttle position sensor) 輸出電壓 (惰轉時) 0.63–0.73 V, and 汽油管路壓力 (惰轉時) 300–390 kPa — all signatures of an EFI system. Idle-mixture adjustment is handled electronically by the ECU + ISC (惰轉轉速控制) (idle-speed control) element and the CO is adjusted via the Yamaha diagnostic tool's CO-adjust mode, not by turning a screw. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (carbureted) and (b) describe the actual fuel system: high-pressure EFI with electric pump (3.0 A draw), MAP-sensor-controlled injection, electric ISC valve managing idle, and CO trim done via the diagnostic tool.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent a float-bowl height or main-jet size — the MWS-150-A has no carburetor, and a fabricated number would mislead the technician into searching for non-existent parts.

The output must not interpret the idle-mixture screw question as a request for ISC step-count adjustment — they are fundamentally different (a screw is mechanical and rider-accessible; the ISC is electronic and dealer-only).
The output must not present air-fuel-ratio adjustment as something done in the workshop with hand tools — CO trim requires the Yamaha diagnostic tool in CO-adjust mode.
The output must not return brake-system or cooling-system content as a substitute answer — the correct response is to explicitly correct the premise and describe the actual EFI / ISC system.

entry.question: What is the recommended two-stroke oil mix ratio for the MWS-150-A, and which type of premix oil should the customer use at the pump?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A is NOT a two-stroke engine — it is a 4-stroke gasoline engine. The 引擎規格 (engine specifications) table explicitly states 燃燒循環 4 行程 (combustion cycle: 4-stroke), 汽缸數 單缸 (single cylinder), 內徑 × 行程 58.0 × 58.7 mm. As a 4-stroke, the engine has a separate 引擎機油 (engine oil) reservoir (capacity 0.90 L at change, 1.00 L disassembled, YAMALUBE SAE 10W-40, API SG or higher, JASO MA/MB) and the fuel tank holds 限用無鉛汽油 (推薦使用 92 無鉛汽油) (unleaded petrol, 92 RON recommended) WITHOUT any oil mix. Pouring two-stroke premix oil into the fuel tank would foul the catalyst, foul the spark plug, and trigger DTCs from the oxygen sensor; pouring fuel-with-mix into a 4-stroke is a customer-education issue. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (two-stroke) and (b) explain that the bike uses straight 92 RON unleaded in the tank and 10W-40 four-stroke oil in the crankcase, with NO mixing.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent a two-stroke oil mix ratio (e.g. '50:1') — the MWS-150-A is a 4-stroke; any mix ratio is fabricated.
The output must not state the engine oil should be poured into the fuel tank — engine oil lives in the crankcase, fuel in the fuel tank, and they are never mixed on a 4-stroke.

The output must not suggest the customer can use 'either two-stroke or four-stroke oil at the pump' — the manual is specific: YAMALUBE 10W-40 4-stroke oil for the crankcase, unleaded 92 RON petrol for the fuel tank.

The output must not return brake-fluid or coolant content as a substitute answer — the correct response is to explicitly correct the premise (no two-stroke, no premix) and describe the 4-stroke lubrication system.

entry.question: On the MWS-150-A, what is the prescribed maintenance interval for inspecting valve clearance, and what are the cold-engine intake and exhaust clearance specifications?
entry.golden_summary:
**Manual default — fixed interval.** The MWS-150-A service manual specifies inspecting and adjusting the valve clearance every 12,000 km on a cold engine — item 4 in the periodic maintenance schedule (廢氣控制系統定期保養表).

**Workshop practice — interval is condition-driven, not fixed.** Per Towngas technician practice (confirmed 2026-05-20), the 12,000 km figure is treated as a default only when the vehicle is in great shape with no other warning signs — which in real workshops is uncommon. The technician's normal workflow is: inspect each vehicle, assess its condition (noises, idle quality, recent diagnostics, accumulated mileage and severity-of-service since last check), and decide a vehicle-specific interval that may be shorter than 12,000 km. The manual's 12,000 km is the upper bound for a healthy vehicle, not a universal rule.

**Clearance specifications — unconditional.** The cold-engine clearance specs themselves are fixed and only valid in 冷機狀態 (cold state): 進氣 (intake) 0.10–0.14 mm (0.0039–0.0055 in) and 排氣 (exhaust) 0.21–0.25 mm (0.0083–0.0098 in). These values come from the engine specifications table and apply regardless of which interval the inspection is performed at.

**Where each fact lives in the manual.** Interval — periodic-maintenance chapter (定期檢查與調整 → 定期保養 → 廢氣控制系統定期保養表). Clearance values — engine specifications table (引擎規格; parser-quirk slug 冷卻系統).

entry.question: Customer brought in their MWS-150-A with the engine warning lamp on. The Yamaha tool reads DTC P0117 and the bike is cold. Before I order a new coolant temp sensor, I want to bench-test the one in the bike. According to the diagnostic procedure for P0117, what wire colour identifies the short-to-ground continuity test, and per the sensor-inspection section, what resistance should the coolant temperature sensor read at 20 °C and at 100 °C?
entry.golden_summary: Two distinct manual sections answer this. (1) The P0117 diagnostic procedure (故障代碼編號 P0117、P0118) prescribes a ground-short continuity check on the 綠色 / 紅色 (green/red) wire — specifically, check continuity between the coolant-temp-sensor connector and ground; any continuity indicates a ground short and the wire harness must be replaced. (2) The sensor-inspection section (冷卻液溫度感知器的檢查) gives the bench-test resistance specs: 20 °C → 2510–2770 Ω, 100 °C → 210–221 Ω. The bench procedure: submerge the sensor in a container of coolant with a thermometer; heat or cool to the target temperature; measure resistance with a multimeter on the sensor terminals (keep terminals dry). If the measured resistance is out of spec at the test temperature, replace the sensor. After installation, the sensor torques to 15 N·m (1.5 kgf·m, 11 lb·ft).

pitfall_directives: The output must not give just one half of the answer — both the wire-colour test point AND the bench-test resistance values are required; a procedural answer with no resistance spec doesn't help the technician decide whether to replace the sensor.
The output must not specify wire colours other than 綠色 / 紅色 (Green/Red) for the P0117 short-to-ground test — using Black/Blue or Pink/Green would point at the wrong DTC family's wire.

The output must not invent resistance values outside the manual's ranges (e.g. '~3000 Ω at room temperature') — the spec is 2510–2770 Ω at 20 °C, narrow enough that an out-of-range value would falsely condemn or pass the part.
The output must not assert the sensor is good 'because the wiring checks out' — the question is a cross-check; both the wiring test AND the resistance bench test are needed before concluding.

entry.question: Customer says their MWS-150-A 'cranks but won't fire'. They've already replaced the spark plug. According to the troubleshooting tree for 'engine won't start / hard starting', what are the major engine-cylinder and electrical-system causes to walk through, and if I measure compression, what does a healthy MWS-150-A engine read at the spec'd cranking speed?
entry.golden_summary: Two manual sections combine for this diagnostic. (1) The 無法起動 / 起動困難 (engine won't start / hard starting) troubleshooting list groups causes by subsystem. Engine / cylinder-head causes: spark plug loose, cylinder head or cylinder loose, cylinder-head or cylinder gasket damaged, cylinder wear/damage, INCORRECT VALVE CLEARANCE, valve sealing poor, valve-to-seat contact bad, valve timing wrong, valve spring failure, valve stuck. Electrical-system causes: dead/faulty battery, fuses (blown/wrong), spark plug gap incorrect / wrong heat range / dirty / damaged electrode or insulator / faulty cap; ignition coil cracked or shorted, faulty plug wire; ECU failure; crankshaft-position-sensor failure; AC-generator-rotor key broken; main switch / wiring open or short / poor ground / loose connector; starter / starter relay / starter clutch failure. (2) The 壓縮壓力的測量 section gives the spec: 1566–2016 kPa at 860 r/min cranking speed (15.7–20.2 kgf/cm², 222.7–286.7 psi at 860 r/min). The two together let the technician triage — check the cheap electrical items first, then escalate to a compression test if no spark/fuel issues are found.

golden_citations.quote: 汽門間隙不正確
曲軸位置感知器故障
ECU 故障

pitfall_directives: The output must not present just the compression spec without the troubleshooting checklist — the customer reported 'cranks but won't fire', which is a symptom-level question that needs the cause list first.
The output must not give a compression value outside the manual's 1566–2016 kPa range — using a generic motorcycle compression value would mislead the bench reading.

The output must not skip the electrical-system causes (battery, fuses, ignition coil, ECU, CKP sensor) and focus only on cylinder-head causes — for a 'cranks but won't fire' presentation, electrical/ignition causes are usually the higher-prior-probability picks.
The output must not introduce DTC-procedural content (P0117 step-by-step) as the answer — the question is about general no-start triage, not a specific stored fault code.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Tests realistic 'customer says X' workflow that's NOT a maintenance-interval lookup (responding to cross-001 review). Two distinct slugs: troubleshooting causal list + compression spec. Quotes verified verbatim against MWS-150-A.md lines 14315, 14368, 14367 (no-start list) and 7627 (compression spec).

entry.question: Bike is overheating per the customer. According to the troubleshooting guide for 過熱 (overheating), what cooling-system causes should I check first? And once I'm into the radiator cap diagnosis, what is the radiator cap valve-opening pressure spec I should confirm with a cap tester?
entry.golden_summary: Two sections combine to drive overheating diagnosis. (1) 過熱 (overheating) troubleshooting lists cooling-system causes in order: low coolant level; radiator damage or leak, faulty radiator cap, bent/damaged radiator fins; water pump damage or failure; thermostat stuck closed; hose damage / improper connection; steel pipe damage / improper connection. Engine-side causes (carbon build-up, incorrect oil level / viscosity / quality) and ignition-side causes (incorrect spark-plug gap / heat range, ECU failure) are also listed but the cooling-system items are first-pass. (2) The radiator-cap valve-opening-pressure spec is found in the engine-specs cooling section (引擎規格 → 冷卻系統): 水箱蓋閥開啟壓力 108.0–137.4 kPa (1.08–1.37 kgf/cm², 15.7–19.9 psi). A cap that opens significantly below this range will release coolant prematurely (boil-over) — replace it. The thermostat valve-opening temperature in the same spec table is 74.0–78.0 °C, with full-open at 90.0 °C and a lift of 7.0 mm.

golden_citations.quote: 冷卻液量過低
水箱蓋故障
恆溫器一直關閉

pitfall_directives: The output must not present just the radiator-cap spec without the troubleshooting causes — a 'customer reports overheating' question needs the cause checklist before the numerical spec.
The output must not invent a radiator-cap pressure outside the 108.0–137.4 kPa window — the cap pressure is narrow enough that wrong values either condemn a good cap or pass a failing one.
The output must not omit the thermostat-stuck-closed cause — it's a common root cause of overheating and is in the manual's first-line cooling-system list.
The output must not present brake-system, fuel-system, or DTC-specific (P0117 wire colours) content as the answer — overheating is a thermal/fluid diagnostic, not an electrical-fault DTC walk.

entry.question: Customer reports the front brake lever feels spongy after I changed the brake fluid. What is the manual's prescribed air-bleed sequence to clear the spongy feel, AND what front-pad wear-limit thickness should I confirm before signing the job off so I'm not handing the bike back with under-limit pads?
entry.golden_summary: Two sections combine for a complete answer. (1) The 液壓煞車系統空氣的釋放 (hydraulic brake system air-bleed) procedure prescribes a strict order: 第 1 步 right front caliper → 第 2 步 left front caliper → 第 3 步 rear caliper. Per caliper, top up the reservoir with DOT 4 fluid, hook a clear hose to the bleed screw, apply the lever, loosen the screw, retighten, release the lever, repeat until no bubbles. After the front bleed, secure the front lever with a strap and confirm the balancer position before bleeding the rear. After cycling the hydraulic unit (ABS check), repeat the bleed. Bleed-screw torques: front 5 N·m, rear 6 N·m. (2) The 車體規格 (body specifications) table gives the front-brake pad spec: 煞車來令片厚度 5.8 mm new, 限制 (wear limit) 1.0 mm — at or below the limit, the entire pad set must be replaced. Verifying both fixes the spongy feel AND ensures the customer isn't getting their bike back with marginal pads.

entry.question: Bike won't start. The Yamaha tool shows DTC P0335. According to the troubleshooting tree for 'engine won't start', is the crankshaft-position sensor a recognised electrical cause for this presentation, AND per the self-diagnostic table, what fail-safe behaviour does P0335 trigger and what does that mean for whether the engine can run at all?
entry.golden_summary: Two sections give a layered answer. (1) The 無法起動 / 起動困難 (engine won't start / hard starting) troubleshooting list explicitly includes 曲軸位置感知器故障 (crankshaft-position sensor failure) under the 電裝系統 (electrical system) → 點火系統 (ignition system) cause group — so yes, P0335 is a recognised cause of no-start, not just an isolated DTC. (2) The 自我診斷功能表 (self-diagnostic function table) row for P0335 lists the project as 曲軸位置感知器 (未收到曲軸位置感知器的任何正常訊號) (no normal signal received from CKP). 機車故障現象 (vehicle symptom): 引擎無法起動 (engine cannot start). 故障防護系統運作 (fail-safe system operation): 無法運轉 (cannot run) — meaning when the ECU can't see the CKP signal, no ignition or injection is commanded and the engine stays dead. Bottom line: P0335 alone fully explains a no-start; check the CKP connector, harness continuity to ECU, sensor installation (incorrect installation is listed as a possible cause), the AC-generator rotor for damage, then the sensor itself, and ECU last.

golden_citations.quote: 曲軸位置感知器故障
曲軸位置感知器
引擎無法起動。
無法運轉。

pitfall_directives: The output must not give just the DTC description from the self-diagnostic table without linking to the no-start troubleshooting tree — the technician's question explicitly mixes a symptom and a fault code.
The output must not assert P0335 is a 'minor' DTC that lets the engine run in limp mode — the manual is explicit: 故障防護系統運作 = 無法運轉 (cannot run), so the engine will not start at all.

The output must not skip the AC-generator-rotor cause — the manual lists 'AC 發電機轉子有瑕疵' as a possible cause of P0335 (a broken rotor key in particular), and a missed-tooth signal looks identical to a sensor fault.
The output must not present content for a different DTC (P0117, P0107) or for an unrelated subsystem (brake / fuel-injection) as the answer.

entry.question: I'm getting DTC P0117 on the MWS-150-A. What does this code mean, and what is the step-by-step diagnostic procedure?
entry.golden_summary: DTC P0117 on the MWS-150-A indicates the 冷卻液溫度感知器 (coolant temperature sensor) reported a 搭鐵短路 (ground short). Its sibling code P0118 covers open-circuit and power-short variants of the same sensor. The associated diagnostic code is 06, and all checks must be performed in 引擎冷機 (engine cold) state — the procedure relies on comparing the tool's displayed temperature against ambient. The 7-step diagnosis is: (1) inspect the coolant temperature sensor connector for proper seating, bent or broken pins; secure or replace the wire harness if a connection fault is found. (2) inspect the ECU connector the same way. (3) test the wire harness for opens and shorts with both connectors disconnected — for P0117 specifically, check continuity between the coolant temperature sensor connector and ground on the 綠色 / 紅色 (green/red) wire; any continuity indicates a ground short and the harness must be replaced. (4) check the sensor's installation — confirm it is neither loose nor over-tightened, reinstalling or replacing as needed. (5) execute diagnostic mode 06 with the engine cold and confirm the displayed temperature is close to ambient air temperature; if it is not, replace the coolant temperature sensor. (6) if all earlier steps come back clean, the ECU itself is at fault — replace per page 8-101. (7) erase the DTC using the 山葉診斷工具 (Yamaha diagnostic tool) and verify the engine warning lamp extinguishes.

golden_citations.quote: [P0117] 冷卻液溫度感知器：偵測到搭鐵短路。
在引擎冷機狀態進行此程序。
[P0117] 搭鐵短路
冷卻液溫度感知器接頭與搭鐵之間:綠色/紅色－搭鐵
更換 ECU。

pitfall_directives: The output must not assert that DTC P0117 involves the oxygen sensor (氧氣感知器) — that is a different family of DTCs (P0130–P0139).
The output must not assert that P0117 is an air-fuel-ratio (空燃比) issue — P0117 is an electrical short-to-ground on the coolant temperature sensor, not a mixture-tuning problem.
The output must not present brake-system or tire-pressure content as the primary answer to this question — P0117 is an engine-management DTC, unrelated to those domains.

The output must not specify a wire colour other than 綠色 / 紅色 (Green/Red) for the P0117 short-to-ground continuity test — using a different colour code (e.g. Black/Blue) would mislead the technician into testing the wrong wire.

entry.question: When setting the MWS-150-A engine to TDC of the compression stroke for valve-clearance adjustment, which alignment marks must be confirmed?  The valve-clearance adjustment section (3-6) shows the camshaft-sprocket alignment directly; the AC-generator-rotor (flywheel) alignment is documented with figures in the cylinder-head section (5-12 / 5-15).  Identify both alignments using the figures in those sections.
entry.golden_summary:
Confirming TDC of the compression stroke requires checking TWO alignment marks across TWO sections of the manual.  Both alignments are visual — the I-shaped marks are short tick lines whose precise position cannot be located from text alone, so the figures must be consulted directly.

(1) **Camshaft side** — shown in the 汽門間隙的調整 (valve-clearance adjustment) section, page 3-6.  The tick mark on the camshaft sprocket must visually align with the matching tick mark on the camshaft stopper plate (refer to figure _page_84_Picture_28).

(2) **Crankshaft side** — shown in the 汽缸頭 (cylinder head) section, pages 5-12 / 5-15.  The I-shaped tick mark on the AC generator rotor (flywheel) must visually align with the matching tick mark on the right crankcase, viewed through the inspection port (refer to figures _page_225_Picture_5 and _page_227_Picture_26).

The crankshaft-side alignment is the canonical way to find TDC; the camshaft-side alignment then disambiguates compression stroke from exhaust stroke (the engine cycles to the same crankshaft position twice per 4-stroke cycle, so the camshaft alignment is what tells you which stroke you're on).

Note on figure labels: the manual marks each pair of alignment marks with figure-local letter callouts (a/b in one figure, c/d in another) — these letters are NOT universal identifiers across the manual, so a self-contained answer must describe the physical positions rather than rely on the letters.

golden_citations.quote: 活塞在壓縮衝程的上死點 (TDC)時,將凸輪軸鏈輪上的記號 "a" 對齊凸輪軸停止器板上的對準記號 "b"。
AC 發電機轉子的 "I" 記號 "a" (對齊右曲軸箱上的對準記號 "b")
活塞在壓縮衝程的 TDC 時,將凸輪軸鏈輪上的 "I" 記號 "c" 對齊凸輪軸停止器板上的對 準記號 "d"。
將 AC 發電機轉子的 "I" 記號 "a" 對齊右 曲軸箱上的對準記號 "b"。

pitfall_directives: The output must not omit one of the two required mark alignments — both the camshaft-sprocket-to-stopper-plate alignment AND the crankshaft-(flywheel)-to-crankcase alignment are mandatory for confirming TDC of compression stroke.

The output must not rely solely on figure-local letter labels (a/b/c/d) to identify which mark goes where — those letters reset per figure and are not universal identifiers; a usable answer must describe the physical positions of the marks.

The output must not omit the requirement that TDC be on the COMPRESSION stroke specifically — TDC of the exhaust stroke is a different position and would lead to incorrect valve-clearance measurements.

The output must not claim the crankshaft-side (AC-generator-rotor / right-crankcase) alignment is documented in the 汽門間隙的調整 section — that figure only lives in the 汽缸頭 (cylinder head) chapter (sections 汽缸頭的拆卸 and 汽缸頭的安裝).
The output must not present DTC P0117 / coolant-temperature-sensor / brake-system content as the answer — those topics are unrelated to camshaft timing alignment.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-10 for HARNESS-17 dashboard image-required bucket.  Rewritten 2026-05-16 in response to Towngas technician feedback (issue #89): (a) self-contained quotes that describe physical positions instead of relying on figure-local letter callouts; (b) corrected slug attribution — only the camshaft-sprocket alignment lives in 汽門間隙的調整; the AC-generator-rotor (crankshaft-side) alignment is documented in the cylinder-head chapter (汽缸頭的拆卸 + 汽缸頭的安裝); (c) figure_image_paths added per citation so the golden card can embed the manual's figure directly (no hyperlink hop).

entry.question: I'm reassembling the front handlebar on a MWS-150-A and need to confirm the cable routing. Per the 鋼索配置 (cable routing) section (page 2-21), what cables / hoses pass behind the handlebar in the front-view, and what is the routing of the throttle accel cable vs decel cable as shown in the figure?
entry.golden_summary: Per 鋼索配置 (cable routing), the 方向把手 (handlebar) front-view + rear-view figure (page 49) shows eleven labelled items passing through the bar area: 1. 油門鋼索 (加速鋼索) (throttle accel cable); 2. 前煞車軟管 (front brake hose); 3. 煞車把手鋼索 (brake-lever cable); 4. 後煞車軟管 (rear brake hose); 5. 後煞車燈開關導線 (rear brake-light switch wire); 6. 左把手開關導線 (left handlebar switch wire); 7. 駐車煞車鋼索 (parking brake cable); 8. 電線總成 (main harness); 9. 右把手開關導線 (right handlebar switch wire); 10. 前煞車燈開關導線 (front brake-light switch wire); 11. 油門鋼索 (減速鋼索) (throttle decel cable). The accel cable and decel cable are routed as items 1 and 11 — they are NOT interchangeable; reversing them gives reversed throttle response and is dangerous. The exact left/right side, over/under sequence, and clamp positions can only be confirmed by reading the actual figures (_page_49_Picture_3.jpeg shows the front + rear handlebar view), because the manual labels positions with numeric callouts rather than full prose.

golden_citations.quote: 方向把手 (前視圖與後視圖)
1. 油門鋼索 (加速鋼索)
11. 油門鋼索 (減速鋼索)
2. 前煞車軟管

pitfall_directives: The output must not omit the accel-vs-decel cable distinction — reversing the two routes the throttle backwards and is a safety-critical mistake; both cables must be identified.
The output must not describe a routing for items the figure does not show on this view (e.g. coolant hoses or fuel hoses are not part of the handlebar routing figure).
The output must not rely solely on the figure-local numeric callouts (1–11) without describing what each item is — the technician needs the part name, not just an index.
The output must not assert the routing can be reconstructed from text alone — the figure is needed for exact left/right and over/under sequencing.

result.question: I'm reassembling the front handlebar on a MWS-150-A and need to confirm the cable routing. result.output_text:
According to the 鋼索配置 (Cable Routing) section, the front-view handlebar diagram shows 11 components. The throttle acceleration cable (油門鋼索 (加速鋼索), item 1) and throttle deceleration cable (油門鋼索 (減速鋼索), item 11) are positioned on opposite sides of the handlebar assembly. Other cables/hoses passing behind the handlebar include: front brake hose (item 2), brake lever cable (item 3), rear brake hose (item 4), rear brake light switch wire (item 5), left handlebar switch wire (item 6), parking brake cable (item 7), wire harness (item 8), right handlebar switch wire (item 9), and front brake light switch wire (item 10).

entry.question: When measuring engine oil level on the MWS-150-A, the manual references low-mark 'a' and high-mark 'b' on the dipstick — but those letters are only meaningful with the figure. Where physically on the dipstick are the min and max marks located, and what should the technician confirm visually before topping up?
entry.golden_summary: Per 引擎機油量的檢查 (engine oil level check), the manual marks the dipstick with two reference notches and labels them in figure-local letters 'a' (lower / minimum) and 'b' (upper / maximum). Physically, these are two horizontal etched lines near the dipstick's submerged end — 'a' is the line closer to the dipstick tip (lower in the crankcase when inserted), 'b' is the line further from the tip (higher in the crankcase). The technician must (1) park the bike on the main stand on level ground and confirm the bike is upright, (2) start and warm the engine for a few minutes then shut off, (3) wait a few minutes for the oil to settle, (4) remove the dipstick — but importantly NOT screw it back in when reading — and (5) confirm the oil level falls between 'a' (min) and 'b' (max). If below 'a', add the recommended oil (YAMALUBE SAE 10W-40, API SG or higher, JASO MA/MB) up to the correct level. The figures _page_98_Picture_17 and _page_98_Picture_18 show the dipstick orientation and the mark positions visually — the precise vertical spacing between 'a' and 'b' is small enough that the figure is needed to avoid mis-reading the level.

golden_citations.quote: 引擎機油量應介於最低油位記號 "a" 與最高 油位記號 "b" 之間。
檢查油位時請不要旋入量油尺。
推薦的品牌 YAMALUBE SAE 黏稠度等級 10W-40

pitfall_directives: The output must not claim the dipstick should be screwed in fully for the reading — the manual explicitly says NOT to screw it in (旋入量油尺), and screwing it in gives a falsely-high reading.
The output must not omit the warm-up + wait-to-settle step — reading on a cold engine or immediately after shutoff causes false-low readings because oil is still in the upper galleries.
The output must not rely solely on figure-local letter labels (a/b) without describing the physical position of the marks — those letters are only valid inside the figure.
The output must not invent a numeric quantity (e.g. 'fill to 250 ml above min') — the manual only specifies the visual range between min and max, not a millilitre delta.

entry.question: During coolant replacement on a MWS-150-A, I need to release trapped air via the dedicated air-release bolt. Where physically on the cooling system is the air-release bolt located, and what does the figure show for the routing — is the bolt on the radiator, the engine, or somewhere else?
entry.golden_summary: Per the 冷卻液的更換 (coolant replacement) section, the 空氣釋放螺栓 (air-release bolt) — labelled '1' in the figure on page 101 — is a small bleed bolt mounted in the upper cooling-system circuit; its exact location is shown in figure _page_101_Picture_31.jpeg (the figure printed immediately under the air-release torque spec). Without the figure, the bolt cannot be physically identified — the manual references it by callout '1' but does not describe its location in prose. The procedure is: after refilling the radiator with the recommended coolant to the specified capacity (0.46 L radiator + all hoses, 0.25 L reserve tank to max mark), remove the air-release bolt to let trapped air escape, then reinstall and torque to 10 N·m (1.0 kgf·m, 7.2 lb·ft). The cylinder-side drain bolt torques to 10 N·m and the radiator-side drain bolt to 1.0 N·m — both with NEW seals (copper washer / O-ring respectively). Verifying the bleed completed properly requires running the engine to operating temperature and topping up the reserve tank to the upper mark.

golden_citations.quote: 拆卸空氣釋放螺栓 "1",釋放冷卻系統內的空 氣。
空氣釋放螺栓 10 N·m (1.0 kgf·m,7.2 lb·ft)
水箱 (含所有管線) 0.46 L (0.49 US qt,0.40 Imp.qt)

entry.question: While bleeding the front brakes on a MWS-150-A, the procedure says to slide the rubber boot left and confirm the balancer 'is in the position shown in the figure'. What is that figure-shown position physically — and why does the balancer's position matter for a successful brake bleed?
entry.golden_summary: Per 液壓煞車系統空氣的釋放 (hydraulic brake system air-release), step (k) reads 「將橡皮套 '1' 往左滑,然後檢查平衡器 '2' 位於圖示位置。」 — slide the rubber boot left, then verify the balancer is in the figure-shown position. Figure _page_92_Picture_24.jpeg shows the balancer in its 'system-balanced' position: it sits centred or in a specific reference position relative to the master cylinder linkage. Without the figure, 'the position shown' has no textual description; the manual relies entirely on the visual reference. The balancer's position matters because the MWS-150-A uses a linked / combined-brake system where the front master cylinder shares hydraulic load with the rear via a balancer; if the balancer is mis-positioned during the bleed, the bleed sequence won't equalise pressure between front and rear circuits and the lever will still feel spongy after a 'successful' looking bleed. After confirming the balancer position, the rear bleed proceeds; once the hydraulic unit (ABS) is cycled, the front + rear bleed steps must be repeated.

entry.question: When measuring CO concentration at idle on the MWS-150-A, the manual says the CO/HC tester sampling probe must be inserted into the exhaust tail pipe to a specific depth and secured with a heat-resistant rubber tube. What is the insertion depth, and visually how should the probe and tube be arranged at the tailpipe per the figure on page 87?
entry.golden_summary: Per the 惰轉排氣的檢查 (idle exhaust inspection) section, the CO/HC tester sampling probe (探針 '2') must be inserted 600 mm into the exhaust tail pipe, and a heat-resistant rubber tube must be installed to secure the probe and seal the joint so no exhaust gas escapes. The figure on page 87 (_page_87_Picture_26.jpeg) shows the physical arrangement: the probe is fed deep into the muffler outlet, the heat-resistant tube wraps the joint between probe and tailpipe to prevent leakage at the sample point. The 600 mm insertion depth and the heat-resistant tube together ensure the CO sample comes from the post-catalyst section of the exhaust at idle, not from ambient air being sucked into the probe at the tip. Prerequisites for the measurement: the diagnostic tachometer is fitted, engine is warmed up so coolant temperature reaches ≥80 °C (176 °F), idle is confirmed at 1500–1700 r/min, and the Yamaha diagnostic tool's diagnostic-code 06 reads true coolant temperature. The CO target is 0.0–1.5 %.

golden_citations.quote: 如圖所示,由於需將取樣探針插入排氣口 600 mm,務必安裝耐熱橡皮管。

entry.question: What is the specified engine idle speed for the MWS-150-A, and at what coolant temperature must idle be measured per the inspection procedure?

entry.question: What cold tire pressures does the MWS-150-A manual specify for the front and rear tires, single rider? Are the values different for two riders?

The manual lists the same values for both 1-rider and 2-rider load conditions — there is no increase for two-up riding on this model. entry.golden_summary: Per the 車體規格 section, the front-brake 煞車來令片厚度 (pad thickness) is 5.8 mm (0.23 in) when new, with a 限制 (wear limit) of 1.0 mm (0.04 in) — at the limit, the entire pad set must be replaced. The specified brake fluid for both front and rear systems is DOT 4. The manual warns that mixing different brake-fluid grades can cause harmful chemical reactions and reduce braking performance.

golden_citations.quote: 煞車來令片厚度 5.8 mm (0.23 in) 限制 1.0 mm (0.04 in)
指定煞車液 DOT 4

pitfall_directives: The output must not specify DOT 3 or DOT 5/5.1 — the MWS-150-A manual is specific about DOT 4; using DOT 5 (silicone-based) in a DOT 4 system would damage seals.

The output must not invent a higher wear-limit value (e.g. 2.0 mm) — the manual sets the limit at 1.0 mm; allowing thicker remaining material to be 'condemned' is technically wrong even if more conservative.
The output must not mention rear-brake pad numbers (5.3 mm / 0.8 mm limit) as if they were front-brake values — the front and rear pads have DIFFERENT standard and limit thicknesses.
The output must not present unrelated content (engine oil, tire pressure) as the answer — the question is specifically about brake pads + brake fluid.

entry.question: I'm getting DTC P0107 on the MWS-150-A. What does this code mean, and what is the step-by-step diagnostic procedure including the specific wire-color checks?
entry.golden_summary: DTC P0107 on the MWS-150-A indicates the 進氣壓力感知器 (intake-pressure sensor) reported an open-circuit or 搭鐵短路 (ground short) — the ECU saw the sensor's circuit voltage at 0.2 V or below. P0108 (its sibling code) covers the open-circuit-or-power-short case. The 7-step procedure: (1) inspect the throttle-body sensor assembly connector for proper seating, bent or broken pins; secure or replace the harness if a connection fault is found. (2) inspect the ECU connector the same way. (3) test the wire harness — for P0107 short-to-ground, check continuity between the throttle-body sensor connector and ground on the 粉紅色 / 綠色 (pink/green) wire; any continuity means a ground-short and the harness must be replaced. For P0107 open-circuit, check continuity throttle-body-sensor-to-ECU connector on the 藍色 – 藍色 (blue – blue) wire and 粉紅色 / 綠色 – 粉紅色 / 綠色 lines. (4) check the throttle-body sensor's installation (loose / over-tight → reinstall). (5) execute 診斷模式 (code 03) and crank the engine; if the displayed value does not change, replace the throttle body. (6) if all earlier steps pass, replace the ECU per page 8-101. (7) erase the DTC with the Yamaha diagnostic tool and verify the engine warning lamp extinguishes.

golden_citations.quote: [P0107] 進氣壓力感知器：偵測到斷路或搭鐵短路。
[P0107] 搭鐵短路
節流閥本體感知器總成接頭和搭鐵之間：粉紅色 / 綠色 – 搭鐵
節流閥本體感知器總成接頭與 ECU 接頭之間：藍色 – 藍色
更換節流閥本體。

pitfall_directives: The output must not assert that DTC P0107 involves the coolant-temperature sensor or oxygen sensor — those are different families (P0117/P0118 and P0130/P0132/P2195 respectively).
The output must not specify a wire colour other than 粉紅色 / 綠色 (Pink/Green) for the P0107 ground-short test — testing the wrong wire would either miss the fault or condemn the wrong harness.
The output must not state that DTC P0107 can be cleared by simply restarting the bike — the manual requires the Yamaha diagnostic tool to erase the DTC and confirm the warning lamp extinguishes.

The output must not skip the connector-inspection steps (items 1 and 2) and jump directly to ECU replacement — the manual deliberately puts connector / harness checks first because intermittent connection faults are the most common root cause.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Parallels dtc-001 (P0117) but on a different sensor family (intake-pressure). Quotes verified verbatim against MWS-150-A.md lines 11966, 11984, 11985, 12000. The wire-colour and step-ordering pitfalls are specific to this sensor and protect against template-style answers that mix up wire colours across DTC families.

entry.question: Walk me through the engine oil-change procedure on the MWS-150-A from drain to reset, including the torque values and refill capacities the manual specifies.
entry.golden_summary: The MWS-150-A engine oil change is a multi-step procedure that starts with a brief warm-up to thin the oil, then drains and refills with the manual's specified torques and capacity. Step-by-step: (1) start the engine, warm for a few minutes, then shut off. (2) place a container under the drain bolt. (3) remove the dipstick and the engine-oil drain bolt (with its gasket). (4) drain the oil completely. (5) (optional) if also cleaning the oil strainer: remove the strainer cover, install a new O-ring (lubricated with lithium-soap grease), clean and check the strainer, reinstall — strainer-cover torque 32 N·m (3.2 kgf·m, 23 lb·ft). (6) reinstall the engine-oil drain bolt with a NEW gasket — torque 20 N·m (2.0 kgf·m, 14 lb·ft). (7) refill the crankcase with the recommended oil to the specified capacity: 0.90 L for an oil change, 1.00 L if disassembled. (8) reinstall the dipstick, restart the engine, warm for a few minutes, shut off. (9) check for leaks. (10) recheck the oil level. (11) (optional) check oil pressure via the oil-check bolt (loosen slightly, idle until oil seeps; check-bolt torque 7 N·m / 0.7 kgf·m / 5.1 lb·ft on reinstall). (12) reset the oil-change indicator on the OIL TRIP meter.

golden_citations.quote: 起動引擎,暖車數分鐘後熄火。
機油濾網蓋 32 N·m (3.2 kgf·m,23 lb·ft)
引擎機油排油螺栓 20 N·m (2.0 kgf·m,14 lb·ft)
機油更換 0.90 L (0.95 US qt,0.79 Imp.qt)
引擎機油檢查螺栓 7 N·m (0.7 kgf·m,5.1 lb·ft)

pitfall_directives: The output must not give a drain-bolt torque other than 20 N·m — over-torquing strips the crankcase threads, under-torquing leaks.
The output must not skip the warm-up step before draining — cold oil does not drain completely and leaves contaminated oil in the engine.
The output must not reuse the old drain-bolt gasket — the manual explicitly calls for a NEW gasket (墊片 New), and reusing it causes weeping leaks.
The output must not omit the OIL TRIP indicator reset (step 14 / final step) — without resetting, the customer's service-reminder counter remains incorrect.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Tests multi-step procedural reconstruction where torque values and capacity must be threaded through narrative correctly. Quotes verified verbatim against MWS-150-A.md lines 3017, 3048, 3062, 3078, 3127. The 'new gasket' pitfall mirrors a common shop shortcut — reusing crush washers — that the manual explicitly forbids.

result.question: Walk me through the engine oil-change procedure on the MWS-150-A from drain to reset, including the torque values and refill capacities the manual specifies.
result.output_text:
The MWS-150-A oil change procedure requires warming the engine first, then draining oil via the drain bolt (torque: 20 N·m/14 lb·ft). Oil capacity is 0.90 L for standard change or 1.00 L if the filter screen is cleaned. After refilling, reset the oil change trip meter by selecting it on the dashboard and holding RESET for 1 second, then 3-4 seconds when OIL CHANGE flashes.

entry.question: Walk me through the coolant change procedure on the MWS-150-A — drain, refill, and air-bleed — with the torque values the manual specifies.
entry.golden_summary: The MWS-150-A coolant change starts with the engine cold (the manual warns hot radiator pressure can cause severe scalding) and follows a strict order. Step-by-step: (1) remove the chest cover, footboard, and bottom cover for access. (2) detach the reserve-tank hose and breather hose; remove the reserve-tank cover and reserve tank. (3) drain coolant from the reserve tank. (4) remove the radiator outer cover. (5) remove the radiator cap — use a towel and turn slowly counter-clockwise to release residual pressure, wait for the hissing to stop, then push down and continue counter-clockwise. (6) remove the radiator-side coolant drain bolt (with O-ring) and the cylinder-side coolant drain bolt (with copper washer). (7) drain coolant from engine and radiator. (8) reinstall both drain bolts with NEW seals — cylinder-side bolt 10 N·m, radiator-side bolt 1.0 N·m. (9) reconnect the reserve-tank hoses. (10) refill the cooling system with the recommended coolant — YAMAHA GENUINE COOLANT, mix ratio 1:1 antifreeze:water, radiator capacity 0.46 L, reserve-tank-to-full-mark 0.25 L. (11) bleed the system air via the air-release bolt — torque 10 N·m on reinstall. (12) reinstall the radiator cap, reserve tank, and outer panels.

entry.question: What is the procedure to bleed air from the MWS-150-A hydraulic brake system, including the ABS sequence and the bleed-screw torques?
entry.golden_summary: The MWS-150-A hydraulic brake bleed follows a strict caliper order — 第 1 步 (Step 1) right front caliper → 第 2 步 (Step 2) left front caliper → 第 3 步 (Step 3) rear caliper. Per caliper, the procedure is: (a) top up the reservoir to the proper level with specified DOT 4 brake fluid and reinstall the diaphragm; (b) attach clear plastic hose to the bleed screw with the other end in an open container; (c) slowly apply the brake lever a few times; (d) fully apply and hold; (e) loosen the bleed screw to release pressure; (f) tighten the bleed screw then release the lever; (g) repeat until no air bubbles appear in the hose. After the front, fully apply the front lever and secure with a strap; slide back the rubber boot and confirm the balancer is in the figure's specified position. Repeat the bleed for the rear caliper. After ABS-cycling the brake (operating the hydraulic unit), repeat the front + rear bleed steps and top up the reservoir. Bleed-screw torques: 前煞車卡鉗空氣釋放螺絲 5 N·m, 後煞車卡鉗空氣釋放螺絲 6 N·m. The manual mandates checking the master switch is OFF before testing the hydraulic unit.

entry.question: How do I measure engine compression on the MWS-150-A, and what does a low reading interpreted via the wet-test (a teaspoon of oil down the spark-plug hole) tell me about engine condition?
entry.golden_summary: The MWS-150-A compression measurement procedure starts with verifying valve clearance is in spec (adjust if not), then warming the engine and removing the spark plug. The compression gauge (90890-03081 or YU-33223) is installed with the extension adapter (90890-04136). The compression spec is 1566–2016 kPa at 860 r/min (15.7–20.2 kgf/cm², 222.7–286.7 psi at 860 r/min). To run: turn the master switch ON, open the throttle fully, crank the engine until the gauge reading stabilises. The wet test diagnoses the root cause if pressure is low: pour a teaspoon of engine oil into the spark-plug hole and re-measure. (a) If the reading is HIGHER than the dry test → piston rings are worn or damaged (oil seals the rings temporarily). (b) If the reading is the SAME as the dry test → the piston, valves, cylinder-head gasket, or piston rings themselves may be defective. If pressure is ABOVE the upper limit, inspect for carbon build-up on cylinder head, valve faces, and piston crown and clean. The spark plug is then reinstalled at 13 N·m. The manual warns that the spark-plug wire must be grounded before cranking to prevent stray sparks (warning EWA12960).

pitfall_directives: The output must not reverse the wet-test interpretation — 'higher with oil' indicates worn rings (oil seals them), 'same with oil' indicates valves/head-gasket; getting this backwards leads to the wrong repair.
The output must not omit the spark-plug-wire-ground safety step (warning EWA12960) — stray spark during crank can ignite fuel vapour at the open plug hole.
The output must not give a compression spec outside 1566–2016 kPa @ 860 r/min — values like 'around 1000 kPa' or generic '150 psi' are wrong for this specific engine.
The output must not skip the valve-clearance pre-check — if valves aren't sealing because of incorrect clearance, the compression reading is misleading and the wet test won't help.

entry.question: What are the gear ratios and shift-fork inspection limits for the MWS-150-A manual transmission, and how often should the clutch cable be adjusted?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A does NOT have a manual transmission, gears, shift forks, or a clutch cable — it uses a V 皮帶 (V-belt) automatic continuously-variable transmission (CVT) with a dry centrifugal clutch (乾式、離心式、片式). The 變速類型 (transmission type) is explicitly listed as 'V 皮帶、自動' (V-belt, automatic) and the gear ratio entry is '齒輪比 2.306–0.725 : 1' — a CONTINUOUS range, not discrete gear ratios, which is the defining signature of a CVT. There is no shift-fork in the engine, no clutch lever or cable for the rider to operate, and no periodic clutch-cable adjustment in the maintenance table. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (manual transmission) and (b) describe the actual drivetrain: V-belt CVT with centrifugal automatic clutch — service items are V-belt wear (limit 23.0 mm down from 25.5 mm), clutch shoe thickness (limit 2.5 mm from 4.0 mm), clutch engagement speed 2200–2600 r/min, and clutch dis-engagement 4700–5300 r/min, all replaced based on the BELT TRIP indicator rather than a calendar interval.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent gear ratios for a manual transmission — the MWS-150-A has none; fabricating numbers would mislead the technician into looking for parts that don't exist.
The output must not present clutch-cable adjustment intervals as if a cable existed — the centrifugal clutch is purely engine-rpm-actuated and has no user-adjustable cable.
The output must not assert the gear ratio '2.306–0.725 : 1' represents two discrete gears — it is the continuous CVT range, low-to-high, not 1st and 2nd gear.

The output must not present brake-system, cooling-system, or unrelated content as a substitute answer — the correct response is to explicitly correct the premise (no manual transmission) and describe the actual CVT drivetrain.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Tests premise-correction: the question is plausible because most motorcycles have manual gearboxes, but the TRICITY 150 / MWS-150-A is a CVT scooter. The CVT gear-ratio range (2.306–0.725:1) is the giveaway — that's a CVT signature, not gears. Empty golden_citations because there is no manual section that legitimately answers a manual-transmission gear-ratio question.

entry.question: What is the turbocharger boost-pressure spec for the MWS-150-A, and what is the wastegate adjustment procedure?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A is NOT turbocharged — it is a 155 cm³, single-cylinder, naturally-aspirated 4-stroke engine with 燃燒循環 4 行程 (4-stroke combustion cycle) and SOHC valve gear. The engine specifications list 起動系統 (starting system) 電動起動器 (electric starter), 推薦的汽油 (recommended fuel) 限用無鉛汽油 (推薦使用 92 無鉛汽油) (unleaded petrol, 92 RON recommended), and a 壓縮比 (compression ratio) of 10.5:1 — a normal naturally-aspirated figure, not a turbo configuration. There is no turbocharger, no wastegate, no charge-air cooler, no boost-pressure sensor. Air enters via the 空氣濾清器 (air filter, wet-paper element) and 節流閥本體 (throttle body) with manifold absolute-pressure sensing for fuel-injection control only — not boost regulation. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (turbocharged) and (b) describe the actual induction: naturally-aspirated, single throttle body, MAP-sensor-based fuel injection with VVA (可變汽門作動器) for valve actuation, idle 1500–1700 r/min.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent a boost-pressure spec — the MWS-150-A is naturally-aspirated; fabricating a turbo boost number is unverifiable and dangerous if a technician acts on it.
The output must not present the MAP sensor (進氣壓力感知器 / intake-pressure sensor) as a boost-pressure sensor — they measure manifold absolute pressure for fuel-injection control, not turbo boost.
The output must not present wastegate adjustment intervals as if a wastegate existed — there is no wastegate on this engine.

The output must not return a generic 'consult the manual' refusal — the correct response is to explicitly correct the premise and describe what the bike actually uses (natural aspiration + VVA + EFI).
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Tests premise correction on a forced-induction claim. Empty golden_citations intentional. The 10.5:1 compression ratio is the giveaway: turbo gas engines typically run 7–9:1; 10.5:1 is a high-compression naturally-aspirated street engine.

entry.question: On the MWS-150-A, what is the correct float-bowl height and idle-mixture-screw adjustment for the carburetor?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A is NOT carbureted — it is electronic-fuel-injected (EFI). There is no carburetor, no float bowl, no idle-mixture screw, no main jet, and no pilot jet to adjust. The fuel-system spec sheet lists 汽油泵浦 (fuel pump) 電氣式 (electric), 噴油嘴 (fuel injector) 電阻 12.2Ω, 節流閥本體 (throttle body) ID 記號 BB81 00, 節流閥位置感知器 (throttle position sensor) 輸出電壓 (惰轉時) 0.63–0.73 V, and 汽油管路壓力 (惰轉時) 300–390 kPa — all signatures of an EFI system. Idle-mixture adjustment is handled electronically by the ECU + ISC (惰轉轉速控制) (idle-speed control) element and the CO is adjusted via the Yamaha diagnostic tool's CO-adjust mode, not by turning a screw. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (carbureted) and (b) describe the actual fuel system: high-pressure EFI with electric pump (3.0 A draw), MAP-sensor-controlled injection, electric ISC valve managing idle, and CO trim done via the diagnostic tool.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent a float-bowl height or main-jet size — the MWS-150-A has no carburetor, and a fabricated number would mislead the technician into searching for non-existent parts.

The output must not interpret the idle-mixture screw question as a request for ISC step-count adjustment — they are fundamentally different (a screw is mechanical and rider-accessible; the ISC is electronic and dealer-only).
The output must not present air-fuel-ratio adjustment as something done in the workshop with hand tools — CO trim requires the Yamaha diagnostic tool in CO-adjust mode.
The output must not return brake-system or cooling-system content as a substitute answer — the correct response is to explicitly correct the premise and describe the actual EFI / ISC system.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Tests premise correction on a fuel-system claim. Empty golden_citations intentional — there is no carburetor section in the manual because the bike is EFI. The 300–390 kPa rail pressure and electric pump are dead giveaways.

entry.question: What is the recommended two-stroke oil mix ratio for the MWS-150-A, and which type of premix oil should the customer use at the pump?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. The MWS-150-A is NOT a two-stroke engine — it is a 4-stroke gasoline engine. The 引擎規格 (engine specifications) table explicitly states 燃燒循環 4 行程 (combustion cycle: 4-stroke), 汽缸數 單缸 (single cylinder), 內徑 × 行程 58.0 × 58.7 mm. As a 4-stroke, the engine has a separate 引擎機油 (engine oil) reservoir (capacity 0.90 L at change, 1.00 L disassembled, YAMALUBE SAE 10W-40, API SG or higher, JASO MA/MB) and the fuel tank holds 限用無鉛汽油 (推薦使用 92 無鉛汽油) (unleaded petrol, 92 RON recommended) WITHOUT any oil mix. Pouring two-stroke premix oil into the fuel tank would foul the catalyst, foul the spark plug, and trigger DTCs from the oxygen sensor; pouring fuel-with-mix into a 4-stroke is a customer-education issue. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise (two-stroke) and (b) explain that the bike uses straight 92 RON unleaded in the tank and 10W-40 four-stroke oil in the crankcase, with NO mixing.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent a two-stroke oil mix ratio (e.g. '50:1') — the MWS-150-A is a 4-stroke; any mix ratio is fabricated.
The output must not state the engine oil should be poured into the fuel tank — engine oil lives in the crankcase, fuel in the fuel tank, and they are never mixed on a 4-stroke.

The output must not suggest the customer can use 'either two-stroke or four-stroke oil at the pump' — the manual is specific: YAMALUBE 10W-40 4-stroke oil for the crankcase, unleaded 92 RON petrol for the fuel tank.

The output must not return brake-fluid or coolant content as a substitute answer — the correct response is to explicitly correct the premise (no two-stroke, no premix) and describe the 4-stroke lubrication system.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Tests premise correction on a 2-stroke claim. 4-stroke is explicit in the engine-spec table (line 787). Empty golden_citations intentional — the bike has no premix system.

entry.question: DTC P9999 is showing on the MWS-150-A and the engine warning lamp is on. What does P9999 mean per the manual's self-diagnostic table, and what is the step-by-step diagnosis procedure?
entry.golden_summary: The premise of this question is incorrect. DTC P9999 is NOT defined in the MWS-150-A self-diagnostic table. The manual's complete list of supported DTCs is: P0106, P0107, P0108, P0112, P0113, P0117, P0118, P0122, P0123, P0132, P0201, P0335, P0351, P0500, P0507, P0511, P0560, P0601, P062F, P1601, P2195, P2645. A genuine no-such-code reading 'P9999' on a Yamaha diagnostic tool means one of three things, none of which match the question's premise: (a) the tool is displaying a non-Yamaha-defined OBD-II generic code that this ECU was never designed to set (the MWS-150-A's ECU only emits the codes above); (b) the tool firmware is reporting a communications-failure placeholder rather than an ECU-stored DTC; (c) the tool is connecting to the wrong ECU or vehicle. A correct answer must (a) recognise the false premise — there is no P9999 procedure in this manual — and (b) recommend verifying tool connection, confirming the bike model matches the tool's profile, re-running the self-diagnostic, and reading whichever supported code from the list above is actually stored.
pitfall_directives: The output must not invent a meaning for P9999 (e.g. 'generic ECU fault', 'system bus error') — the manual does not define it, and any meaning would be fabrication.
The output must not present the P0117 or P0335 diagnostic procedure as if it applied to P9999 — those are different codes with their own sections.
The output must not assert that 'all DTCs starting with P9' are catalyst-related — that is OBD-II car convention but does not apply to this Yamaha ECU.

The output must not return a refusal that says only 'I don't know what P9999 is' — the correct response includes the actual list of codes the manual DOES support, so the technician can re-read the tool and pick the real DTC.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Tests refusal + helpful redirect for a fake DTC. Empty golden_citations because no manual section legitimately answers a P9999 question. The supported-code list comes from the Appendix DTC Index at MWS-150-A.md line 14871+ — the answer should surface that list so the technician can re-read the tool and find the actual stored code.

entry.question: On the MWS-150-A, what is the prescribed maintenance interval for inspecting valve clearance, and what are the cold-engine intake and exhaust clearance specifications?
entry.golden_summary:
**Manual default — fixed interval.** The MWS-150-A service manual specifies inspecting and adjusting the valve clearance every 12,000 km on a cold engine — item 4 in the periodic maintenance schedule (廢氣控制系統定期保養表).

**Workshop practice — interval is condition-driven, not fixed.** Per Towngas technician practice (confirmed 2026-05-20), the 12,000 km figure is treated as a default only when the vehicle is in great shape with no other warning signs — which in real workshops is uncommon. The technician's normal workflow is: inspect each vehicle, assess its condition (noises, idle quality, recent diagnostics, accumulated mileage and severity-of-service since last check), and decide a vehicle-specific interval that may be shorter than 12,000 km. The manual's 12,000 km is the upper bound for a healthy vehicle, not a universal rule.

**Clearance specifications — unconditional.** The cold-engine clearance specs themselves are fixed and only valid in 冷機狀態 (cold state): 進氣 (intake) 0.10–0.14 mm (0.0039–0.0055 in) and 排氣 (exhaust) 0.21–0.25 mm (0.0083–0.0098 in). These values come from the engine specifications table and apply regardless of which interval the inspection is performed at.

**Where each fact lives in the manual.** Interval — periodic-maintenance chapter (定期檢查與調整 → 定期保養 → 廢氣控制系統定期保養表). Clearance values — engine specifications table (引擎規格; parser-quirk slug 冷卻系統).

pitfall_directives: The output must reference the manual's 12,000 km figure as the documented interval — omitting that figure (or replacing it without flagging that 12,000 km is the manual's value) is wrong. A correct answer cites 12,000 km AND, ideally, contextualises it as the manual's default for a healthy vehicle.

The output must not give only one half of the answer (e.g., only the spec, or only the interval) — the question is a cross-section ask and a correct answer requires combining facts from two distinct manual sections.
The output must not assert that valve clearance may be measured with a warm engine — the manual mandates 冷機狀態 (cold state) for both measurement and adjustment.
The output must not present DTC P0117 / coolant-temperature-sensor content as the answer — that topic is unrelated to a maintenance-interval lookup for valve clearance.

The output must not present the 12,000 km interval as a strict universal rule with no qualification. Per Towngas workshop practice (issue #89), technicians use a condition-driven per-vehicle interval that may be shorter than 12,000 km; the manual's figure is the default only for a vehicle in great shape (uncommon in practice).

entry.question: Customer brought in their MWS-150-A with the engine warning lamp on. The Yamaha tool reads DTC P0117 and the bike is cold. Before I order a new coolant temp sensor, I want to bench-test the one in the bike. According to the diagnostic procedure for P0117, what wire colour identifies the short-to-ground continuity test, and per the sensor-inspection section, what resistance should the coolant temperature sensor read at 20 °C and at 100 °C?
entry.golden_summary: Two distinct manual sections answer this. (1) The P0117 diagnostic procedure (故障代碼編號 P0117、P0118) prescribes a ground-short continuity check on the 綠色 / 紅色 (green/red) wire — specifically, check continuity between the coolant-temp-sensor connector and ground; any continuity indicates a ground short and the wire harness must be replaced. (2) The sensor-inspection section (冷卻液溫度感知器的檢查) gives the bench-test resistance specs: 20 °C → 2510–2770 Ω, 100 °C → 210–221 Ω. The bench procedure: submerge the sensor in a container of coolant with a thermometer; heat or cool to the target temperature; measure resistance with a multimeter on the sensor terminals (keep terminals dry). If the measured resistance is out of spec at the test temperature, replace the sensor. After installation, the sensor torques to 15 N·m (1.5 kgf·m, 11 lb·ft).

The output must not specify wire colours other than 綠色 / 紅色 (Green/Red) for the P0117 short-to-ground test — using Black/Blue or Pink/Green would point at the wrong DTC family's wire.

The output must not invent resistance values outside the manual's ranges (e.g. '~3000 Ω at room temperature') — the spec is 2510–2770 Ω at 20 °C, narrow enough that an out-of-range value would falsely condemn or pass the part.
The output must not assert the sensor is good 'because the wiring checks out' — the question is a cross-check; both the wiring test AND the resistance bench test are needed before concluding.

entry.question: Customer says their MWS-150-A 'cranks but won't fire'. They've already replaced the spark plug. According to the troubleshooting tree for 'engine won't start / hard starting', what are the major engine-cylinder and electrical-system causes to walk through, and if I measure compression, what does a healthy MWS-150-A engine read at the spec'd cranking speed?
entry.golden_summary: Two manual sections combine for this diagnostic. (1) The 無法起動 / 起動困難 (engine won't start / hard starting) troubleshooting list groups causes by subsystem. Engine / cylinder-head causes: spark plug loose, cylinder head or cylinder loose, cylinder-head or cylinder gasket damaged, cylinder wear/damage, INCORRECT VALVE CLEARANCE, valve sealing poor, valve-to-seat contact bad, valve timing wrong, valve spring failure, valve stuck. Electrical-system causes: dead/faulty battery, fuses (blown/wrong), spark plug gap incorrect / wrong heat range / dirty / damaged electrode or insulator / faulty cap; ignition coil cracked or shorted, faulty plug wire; ECU failure; crankshaft-position-sensor failure; AC-generator-rotor key broken; main switch / wiring open or short / poor ground / loose connector; starter / starter relay / starter clutch failure. (2) The 壓縮壓力的測量 section gives the spec: 1566–2016 kPa at 860 r/min cranking speed (15.7–20.2 kgf/cm², 222.7–286.7 psi at 860 r/min). The two together let the technician triage — check the cheap electrical items first, then escalate to a compression test if no spark/fuel issues are found.

golden_citations.quote: 汽門間隙不正確
曲軸位置感知器故障
ECU 故障

pitfall_directives: The output must not present just the compression spec without the troubleshooting checklist — the customer reported 'cranks but won't fire', which is a symptom-level question that needs the cause list first.
The output must not give a compression value outside the manual's 1566–2016 kPa range — using a generic motorcycle compression value would mislead the bench reading.

The output must not skip the electrical-system causes (battery, fuses, ignition coil, ECU, CKP sensor) and focus only on cylinder-head causes — for a 'cranks but won't fire' presentation, electrical/ignition causes are usually the higher-prior-probability picks.
The output must not introduce DTC-procedural content (P0117 step-by-step) as the answer — the question is about general no-start triage, not a specific stored fault code.

Two distinct slugs: troubleshooting causal list + compression spec. Quotes verified verbatim against MWS-150-A.md lines 14315, 14368, 14367 (no-start list) and 7627 (compression spec).

entry.question: Bike is overheating per the customer. According to the troubleshooting guide for 過熱 (overheating), what cooling-system causes should I check first? And once I'm into the radiator cap diagnosis, what is the radiator cap valve-opening pressure spec I should confirm with a cap tester?
entry.golden_summary: Two sections combine to drive overheating diagnosis. (1) 過熱 (overheating) troubleshooting lists cooling-system causes in order: low coolant level; radiator damage or leak, faulty radiator cap, bent/damaged radiator fins; water pump damage or failure; thermostat stuck closed; hose damage / improper connection; steel pipe damage / improper connection. Engine-side causes (carbon build-up, incorrect oil level / viscosity / quality) and ignition-side causes (incorrect spark-plug gap / heat range, ECU failure) are also listed but the cooling-system items are first-pass. (2) The radiator-cap valve-opening-pressure spec is found in the engine-specs cooling section (引擎規格 → 冷卻系統): 水箱蓋閥開啟壓力 108.0–137.4 kPa (1.08–1.37 kgf/cm², 15.7–19.9 psi). A cap that opens significantly below this range will release coolant prematurely (boil-over) — replace it. The thermostat valve-opening temperature in the same spec table is 74.0–78.0 °C, with full-open at 90.0 °C and a lift of 7.0 mm.

golden_citations.quote: 冷卻液量過低
水箱蓋故障
恆溫器一直關閉

pitfall_directives: The output must not present just the radiator-cap spec without the troubleshooting causes — a 'customer reports overheating' question needs the cause checklist before the numerical spec.
The output must not invent a radiator-cap pressure outside the 108.0–137.4 kPa window — the cap pressure is narrow enough that wrong values either condemn a good cap or pass a failing one.
The output must not omit the thermostat-stuck-closed cause — it's a common root cause of overheating and is in the manual's first-line cooling-system list.
The output must not present brake-system, fuel-system, or DTC-specific (P0117 wire colours) content as the answer — overheating is a thermal/fluid diagnostic, not an electrical-fault DTC walk.

Quotes verified verbatim against MWS-150-A.md lines 14502-14511 (overheating causes) and 813, 815 (cooling specs).

entry.question: Customer reports the front brake lever feels spongy after I changed the brake fluid. What is the manual's prescribed air-bleed sequence to clear the spongy feel, AND what front-pad wear-limit thickness should I confirm before signing the job off so I'm not handing the bike back with under-limit pads?
entry.golden_summary: Two sections combine for a complete answer. (1) The 液壓煞車系統空氣的釋放 (hydraulic brake system air-bleed) procedure prescribes a strict order: 第 1 步 right front caliper → 第 2 步 left front caliper → 第 3 步 rear caliper. Per caliper, top up the reservoir with DOT 4 fluid, hook a clear hose to the bleed screw, apply the lever, loosen the screw, retighten, release the lever, repeat until no bubbles. After the front bleed, secure the front lever with a strap and confirm the balancer position before bleeding the rear. After cycling the hydraulic unit (ABS check), repeat the bleed. Bleed-screw torques: front 5 N·m, rear 6 N·m. (2) The 車體規格 (body specifications) table gives the front-brake pad spec: 煞車來令片厚度 5.8 mm new, 限制 (wear limit) 1.0 mm — at or below the limit, the entire pad set must be replaced. Verifying both fixes the spongy feel AND ensures the customer isn't getting their bike back with marginal pads.

golden_citations.slug: 液壓煞車系統空氣的釋放

entry.question: Bike won't start. The Yamaha tool shows DTC P0335. According to the troubleshooting tree for 'engine won't start', is the crankshaft-position sensor a recognised electrical cause for this presentation, AND per the self-diagnostic table, what fail-safe behaviour does P0335 trigger and what does that mean for whether the engine can run at all?
entry.golden_summary: Two sections give a layered answer. (1) The 無法起動 / 起動困難 (engine won't start / hard starting) troubleshooting list explicitly includes 曲軸位置感知器故障 (crankshaft-position sensor failure) under the 電裝系統 (electrical system) → 點火系統 (ignition system) cause group — so yes, P0335 is a recognised cause of no-start, not just an isolated DTC. (2) The 自我診斷功能表 (self-diagnostic function table) row for P0335 lists the project as 曲軸位置感知器 (未收到曲軸位置感知器的任何正常訊號) (no normal signal received from CKP). 機車故障現象 (vehicle symptom): 引擎無法起動 (engine cannot start). 故障防護系統運作 (fail-safe system operation): 無法運轉 (cannot run) — meaning when the ECU can't see the CKP signal, no ignition or injection is commanded and the engine stays dead. Bottom line: P0335 alone fully explains a no-start; check the CKP connector, harness continuity to ECU, sensor installation (incorrect installation is listed as a possible cause), the AC-generator rotor for damage, then the sensor itself, and ECU last.

golden_citations.quote: 曲軸位置感知器故障
曲軸位置感知器
引擎無法起動。
無法運轉。

pitfall_directives: The output must not give just the DTC description from the self-diagnostic table without linking to the no-start troubleshooting tree — the technician's question explicitly mixes a symptom and a fault code.
The output must not assert P0335 is a 'minor' DTC that lets the engine run in limp mode — the manual is explicit: 故障防護系統運作 = 無法運轉 (cannot run), so the engine will not start at all.

The output must not skip the AC-generator-rotor cause — the manual lists 'AC 發電機轉子有瑕疵' as a possible cause of P0335 (a broken rotor key in particular), and a missed-tooth signal looks identical to a sensor fault.
The output must not present content for a different DTC (P0117, P0107) or for an unrelated subsystem (brake / fuel-injection) as the answer.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Two distinct slugs across troubleshooting + self-diagnostic table. Quotes verified verbatim against MWS-150-A.md lines 14368 (no-start CKP cause), 14703 (P0335 row description, symptom, fail-safe).

entry.question: I'm getting DTC P0117 on the MWS-150-A. What does this code mean, and what is the step-by-step diagnostic procedure?
entry.golden_summary: DTC P0117 on the MWS-150-A indicates the 冷卻液溫度感知器 (coolant temperature sensor) reported a 搭鐵短路 (ground short). Its sibling code P0118 covers open-circuit and power-short variants of the same sensor. The associated diagnostic code is 06, and all checks must be performed in 引擎冷機 (engine cold) state — the procedure relies on comparing the tool's displayed temperature against ambient. The 7-step diagnosis is: (1) inspect the coolant temperature sensor connector for proper seating, bent or broken pins; secure or replace the wire harness if a connection fault is found. (2) inspect the ECU connector the same way. (3) test the wire harness for opens and shorts with both connectors disconnected — for P0117 specifically, check continuity between the coolant temperature sensor connector and ground on the 綠色 / 紅色 (green/red) wire; any continuity indicates a ground short and the harness must be replaced. (4) check the sensor's installation — confirm it is neither loose nor over-tightened, reinstalling or replacing as needed. (5) execute diagnostic mode 06 with the engine cold and confirm the displayed temperature is close to ambient air temperature; if it is not, replace the coolant temperature sensor. (6) if all earlier steps come back clean, the ECU itself is at fault — replace per page 8-101. (7) erase the DTC using the 山葉診斷工具 (Yamaha diagnostic tool) and verify the engine warning lamp extinguishes.

golden_citations.quote: [P0117] 冷卻液溫度感知器：偵測到搭鐵短路。
在引擎冷機狀態進行此程序。
[P0117] 搭鐵短路
冷卻液溫度感知器接頭與搭鐵之間:綠色/紅色－搭鐵
更換 ECU。

pitfall_directives: The output must not assert that DTC P0117 involves the oxygen sensor (氧氣感知器) — that is a different family of DTCs (P0130–P0139).
The output must not assert that P0117 is an air-fuel-ratio (空燃比) issue — P0117 is an electrical short-to-ground on the coolant temperature sensor, not a mixture-tuning problem.
The output must not present brake-system or tire-pressure content as the primary answer to this question — P0117 is an engine-management DTC, unrelated to those domains.

The output must not specify a wire colour other than 綠色 / 紅色 (Green/Red) for the P0117 short-to-ground continuity test — using a different colour code (e.g. Black/Blue) would mislead the technician into testing the wrong wire.

entry.question: When setting the MWS-150-A engine to TDC of the compression stroke for valve-clearance adjustment, which alignment marks must be confirmed?  The valve-clearance adjustment section (3-6) shows the camshaft-sprocket alignment directly; the AC-generator-rotor (flywheel) alignment is documented with figures in the cylinder-head section (5-12 / 5-15).  Identify both alignments using the figures in those sections.
entry.golden_summary:
Confirming TDC of the compression stroke requires checking TWO alignment marks across TWO sections of the manual.  Both alignments are visual — the I-shaped marks are short tick lines whose precise position cannot be located from text alone, so the figures must be consulted directly.

(1) **Camshaft side** — shown in the 汽門間隙的調整 (valve-clearance adjustment) section, page 3-6.  The tick mark on the camshaft sprocket must visually align with the matching tick mark on the camshaft stopper plate (refer to figure _page_84_Picture_28).

(2) **Crankshaft side** — shown in the 汽缸頭 (cylinder head) section, pages 5-12 / 5-15.  The I-shaped tick mark on the AC generator rotor (flywheel) must visually align with the matching tick mark on the right crankcase, viewed through the inspection port (refer to figures _page_225_Picture_5 and _page_227_Picture_26).

The crankshaft-side alignment is the canonical way to find TDC; the camshaft-side alignment then disambiguates compression stroke from exhaust stroke (the engine cycles to the same crankshaft position twice per 4-stroke cycle, so the camshaft alignment is what tells you which stroke you're on).

Note on figure labels: the manual marks each pair of alignment marks with figure-local letter callouts (a/b in one figure, c/d in another) — these letters are NOT universal identifiers across the manual, so a self-contained answer must describe the physical positions rather than rely on the letters.

golden_citations.quote: 活塞在壓縮衝程的上死點 (TDC)時,將凸輪軸鏈輪上的記號 "a" 對齊凸輪軸停止器板上的對準記號 "b"。
AC 發電機轉子的 "I" 記號 "a" (對齊右曲軸箱上的對準記號 "b")
活塞在壓縮衝程的 TDC 時,將凸輪軸鏈輪上的 "I" 記號 "c" 對齊凸輪軸停止器板上的對 準記號 "d"。
將 AC 發電機轉子的 "I" 記號 "a" 對齊右 曲軸箱上的對準記號 "b"。

pitfall_directives: The output must not omit one of the two required mark alignments — both the camshaft-sprocket-to-stopper-plate alignment AND the crankshaft-(flywheel)-to-crankcase alignment are mandatory for confirming TDC of compression stroke.

The output must not rely solely on figure-local letter labels (a/b/c/d) to identify which mark goes where — those letters reset per figure and are not universal identifiers; a usable answer must describe the physical positions of the marks.

The output must not omit the requirement that TDC be on the COMPRESSION stroke specifically — TDC of the exhaust stroke is a different position and would lead to incorrect valve-clearance measurements.

The output must not claim the crankshaft-side (AC-generator-rotor / right-crankcase) alignment is documented in the 汽門間隙的調整 section — that figure only lives in the 汽缸頭 (cylinder head) chapter (sections 汽缸頭的拆卸 and 汽缸頭的安裝).
The output must not present DTC P0117 / coolant-temperature-sensor / brake-system content as the answer — those topics are unrelated to camshaft timing alignment.

entry.question: I'm reassembling the front handlebar on a MWS-150-A and need to confirm the cable routing. Per the 鋼索配置 (cable routing) section (page 2-21), what cables / hoses pass behind the handlebar in the front-view, and what is the routing of the throttle accel cable vs decel cable as shown in the figure?
entry.golden_summary: Per 鋼索配置 (cable routing), the 方向把手 (handlebar) front-view + rear-view figure (page 49) shows eleven labelled items passing through the bar area: 1. 油門鋼索 (加速鋼索) (throttle accel cable); 2. 前煞車軟管 (front brake hose); 3. 煞車把手鋼索 (brake-lever cable); 4. 後煞車軟管 (rear brake hose); 5. 後煞車燈開關導線 (rear brake-light switch wire); 6. 左把手開關導線 (left handlebar switch wire); 7. 駐車煞車鋼索 (parking brake cable); 8. 電線總成 (main harness); 9. 右把手開關導線 (right handlebar switch wire); 10. 前煞車燈開關導線 (front brake-light switch wire); 11. 油門鋼索 (減速鋼索) (throttle decel cable). The accel cable and decel cable are routed as items 1 and 11 — they are NOT interchangeable; reversing them gives reversed throttle response and is dangerous. The exact left/right side, over/under sequence, and clamp positions can only be confirmed by reading the actual figures (_page_49_Picture_3.jpeg shows the front + rear handlebar view), because the manual labels positions with numeric callouts rather than full prose.

golden_citations.quote: 方向把手 (前視圖與後視圖)
1. 油門鋼索 (加速鋼索)
11. 油門鋼索 (減速鋼索)
2. 前煞車軟管

pitfall_directives: The output must not omit the accel-vs-decel cable distinction — reversing the two routes the throttle backwards and is a safety-critical mistake; both cables must be identified.
The output must not describe a routing for items the figure does not show on this view (e.g. coolant hoses or fuel hoses are not part of the handlebar routing figure).
The output must not rely solely on the figure-local numeric callouts (1–11) without describing what each item is — the technician needs the part name, not just an index.
The output must not assert the routing can be reconstructed from text alone — the figure is needed for exact left/right and over/under sequencing.
entry.notes: human-authored 2026-05-16 for HARNESS-18 batch. Image-required because exact routing geometry only lives in the figures, not text. Quotes verified verbatim against MWS-150-A.md lines 1206, 1212, 1222, 1213. Figure path follows the same convention as image-001's _page_NN_Picture_MM.jpeg pattern. Self-contained narrative addresses the image-001 review feedback by describing physical positions instead of relying solely on figure-local numbers.
